title: Reword error status message for misconfigured worker
summary: Issue asks to reword the BlockedStatus shown when a worker has no roles. The proposed new wording is ALREADY implemented in src/coordinated_workers/worker.py:258 (committed 2026-01-14 via PR #126 'fix: error status for misconfigured worker'). Current message: "Node offline: no role assigned. Please configure this worker to enable a role. For example: 'juju config {app} role-all=true'." This matches the issue's suggested alternative almost verbatim; only the example differs (code uses role-all=true vs issue's role-<some valid role>=true). The issue is effectively obsolete and should be verified/closed (optionally tweak the example).
why_good_first_issue: Labelled 'good first issue'; single-line string change at most. Great first task: a newcomer learns the codebase by confirming the reword already landed and either closing the issue or adjusting the example string. No logic, low risk.
repro_steps: grep -rn 'no role assigned' src/ -> src/coordinated_workers/worker.py:258; git log -1 -S 'Node offline: no role assigned' -- src/coordinated_workers/worker.py -> 7482770 (2026-01-14) 'fix: error status for misconfigured worker (#126)'; grep across tests/ for the message -> no test asserts it.
repro_result: CONFIRMED already-implemented/obsolete. worker.py:258 reads exactly: BlockedStatus(f"Node offline: no role assigned. Please configure this worker to enable a role. For example: 'juju config {self._charm.app.name} role-all=true'.") -- i.e. the reworded text from the issue is live. Introduced by commit 7482770 (PR #126, 2026-01-14). No unit test references the string (grep returned nothing).
fix_hint: Either close the issue as already-resolved, or if the maintainers prefer the issue's exact example, change 'role-all=true' to 'role-<some valid role>=true' on worker.py:258. No tests reference the string, so no test updates required.
notes: Unit env: `tox -e fetch-libs` then `tox -e unit` => 166 passed (90% coverage). The unit env builds cleanly only AFTER fetch-libs (otherwise ModuleNotFoundError: charms). This issue is a good candidate precisely because verifying-and-closing is ideal newcomer work, but flag to orchestrator that it may already be done.

title: Dead '.nginx' lazy-module entries in package __init__ after migration to charmlibs.nginx_k8s (raise ModuleNotFoundError at runtime)
suggested_labels: Type: Bug; good first issue; Type: Cleanup
summary: PR #150 (commit 31265e9, 'feat!: migrate nginx module to charmlibs.nginx_k8s') deleted src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py, but src/coordinated_workers/__init__.py still maps four public names to that removed module via _LazyModule('.nginx'): NginxConfig (line 39), Nginx (line 40), NginxPrometheusExporter (line 41), NginxTracingConfig (line 44). They are also still advertised in __all__ (lines 10-15). Because _LazyModule imports on attribute access, `from coordinated_workers import NginxConfig` then using it raises ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'coordinated_workers.nginx' at runtime. The README (line 8) also still links the deleted file: `[NginxConfig](src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py)` -> broken GitHub link.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained, no domain knowledge needed: remove the four dead lazy entries + corresponding __all__ names (or re-point them to the new charmlibs.nginx_k8s package if they should remain re-exported), and fix the README link/description. Clear reproduction, small diff, easy to test.
repro_steps: 1) ls src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py -> No such file. 2) git log --all -- src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py -> removed in 31265e9 (PR #150). 3) PYTHONPATH=src python -c 'import coordinated_workers as cw; cw.NginxConfig.anything' . 4) grep -n NginxConfig README.md -> stale relative link to src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py.
repro_result: REPRODUCED at runtime. Accessing each name raised: "NginxConfig access FAILED: ModuleNotFoundError -> No module named 'coordinated_workers.nginx'" and identically for Nginx, NginxPrometheusExporter, NginxTracingConfig. File src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py confirmed absent; removed by commit 31265e9 (PR #150 'feat!: migrate nginx module to charmlibs.nginx_k8s'). __init__.py lines 39/40/41/44 still reference _LazyModule('.nginx'); __all__ still lists these names. README.md:8 still links the deleted nginx.py.
fix_hint: In src/coordinated_workers/__init__.py remove (or re-point to charmlibs.nginx_k8s) the four _LazyModule('.nginx') assignments and the matching __all__ entries (Nginx, NginxConfig, NginxPrometheusExporter, NginxTracingConfig). In README.md:8 either drop the NginxConfig bullet or update it to reference the charmlibs.nginx_k8s package instead of the removed src/coordinated_workers/nginx.py. Add a tiny import test if desired.
notes: Verified against current main (depth-80 clone). The _LazyModule pattern hides the breakage from a plain `import coordinated_workers`, so it only surfaces on attribute access -- good teaching example. Unit suite (166 tests) does not exercise these names. No outbound writes performed.
